description:
You are a build-engineer who has just received a fresh export of artifact metadata and need to make it usable by an automated release pipeline.

Current project layout  
/home/user/build/  
├── inventory.csv   <-- already present  
└── (nothing else)

inventory.csv is a comma-separated file with exactly three data rows and a single header row:

artifact,version,sha256  
core-lib,1.2.3,abc123def456  
ui-module,2.0.0-rc1,789abc012def  
cli-tool,0.9.8,fedcba987654  

Your tasks:

1. Convert /home/user/build/inventory.csv into a JSON file named /home/user/build/latest.json that meets *all* of the following rules.  
   • The file must contain a single JSON array.  
   • Each row (excluding the header) becomes one JSON object with three string keys:  
     artifact, version, sha256 (all lower-case).  
   • The array must be pretty-printed with an indentation of exactly two spaces.  
   • Objects must appear in the same order as the source rows (core-lib first, ui-module second, cli-tool third).  
   • The file must end with a single trailing newline (Unix LF).  
   Here is the precise, byte-for-byte content the pipeline expects:

[
  {
    "artifact": "core-lib",
    "version": "1.2.3",
    "sha256": "abc123def456"
  },
  {
    "artifact": "ui-module",
    "version": "2.0.0-rc1",
    "sha256": "789abc012def"
  },
  {
    "artifact": "cli-tool",
    "version": "0.9.8",
    "sha256": "fedcba987654"
  }
]

2. Create a plain-text log file at /home/user/build/convert.log containing exactly one line:

inventory.csv converted to latest.json: OK

   • The line must end with a single Unix LF (no extra blank lines).

Constraints & tips:
• You can use any CLI tooling that is available (e.g., awk, sed, python, node, jq, etc.), but keep it to a handful of commands.  
• Do not overwrite inventory.csv.  
• Ensure both new files are group- and world-readable (chmod 644 is fine).  
• The automated grader will compare latest.json and convert.log *byte-for-byte*, so match the formatting exactly—including spacing, commas, newlines and the order of keys inside each JSON object.

When you are done, you should have:

/home/user/build/latest.json  (with the JSON shown above)  
/home/user/build/convert.log  (with the single confirmation line)

No additional files should be created.
truth:
Pre-existing file (already on disk before the agent starts):
Path: /home/user/build/inventory.csv  
Contents (including trailing newline):
artifact,version,sha256
core-lib,1.2.3,abc123def456
ui-module,2.0.0-rc1,789abc012def
cli-tool,0.9.8,fedcba987654

Files that **must** exist after successful completion:

1) /home/user/build/latest.json  
Exact contents (with final LF):

[
  {
    "artifact": "core-lib",
    "version": "1.2.3",
    "sha256": "abc123def456"
  },
  {
    "artifact": "ui-module",
    "version": "2.0.0-rc1",
    "sha256": "789abc012def"
  },
  {
    "artifact": "cli-tool",
    "version": "0.9.8",
    "sha256": "fedcba987654"
  }
]

File mode must be 644.

2) /home/user/build/convert.log  
Exact contents (with final LF):

inventory.csv converted to latest.json: OK

File mode must be 644.
